description: Pre-registered expectations for the conventional replication and prediction contest. Revised 2026-07-13 after the first contest run; see revisions.

revisions.factor_calibration: Second run: wOBA currency reproduced (Nothing 10.05 vs paper 10.22; meso best at 7.22) but the run-factor ordering still failed (meso 5.62 vs FG 5.45). Remaining asymmetry: BR/FG predict in factor units natively while mesoball's run-value effect was converted at 1:1 elasticity, though run scoring is convex (the measured effect-to-wOBA slope is 1.112, not 1.0). Third run gives mesoball the same in-sample unit calibration ((tf-1) regressed on effect) that the factor-native systems get implicitly. Committed in advance: whatever ordering the third run produces is what the paper reports; no further predictor changes.
revisions.meso_predictor: First run used a placeholder trailing EWMA, which smears across regime breaks; it lost the ordering gate to FG (5.66 vs 5.51). Replaced with the manuscript's stated method: real-time changepoint detection on data through Y-1 (same segment routine and thresholds as the Stage 3b screen), predicting the current regime's mean EB-shrunk toward zero with prior variance equal to the between-park variance (8 pts)^2. BR reproduced to the hundredth (5.69 vs 5.70) on the first run, so the harness itself is sound.
revisions.woba_target: First run scored the wOBA currency against the skill-adjusted effect; every system including Nothing came in far under the paper (Nothing 7.74 vs 10.22), proving a target mismatch rather than predictor differences. Replaced with the manuscript's stated definition: raw park wOBA differential vs the league-component mean, with a single in-sample calibration slope mapping run-factor predictions into wOBA-differential units (a scale constant only; identical treatment for all systems).

notes: Substantive gates (FAIL-level): mesoball <= FG <= BR on run-factor MAE, and persistence worse than nothing. Cell deviations from Table 4 are WARN-level findings that correct the paper. If the regime-based mesoball predictor still loses to FG, the paper's 'edges out FanGraphs' sentence is rewritten to match the rerun, and Table 4 is updated either way. The 0.94 published-values correlation needs data\published_factors.csv (season, team, factor).
